org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.commandbase.subsystems.swerve.encoders

Class SwerveEncoderFactory

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.commandbase.subsystems.swerve.encoders.SwerveEncoderFactory

  • public class SwerveEncoderFactory
extends java.lang.Object
    Factory for creating swerve encoders based on configuration.

    This factory creates the appropriate encoder implementation (OctoQuad or Analog) based on the constants configured in Constants.

    Encoder Types:

    • OctoQuad (Digital): High-precision digital encoders via OctoQuad
    • Analog (SRS Hub): Analog potentiometers/magnetic encoders via SRS Hub

    Configuration:

    Set the following constants in Constants.java:

     // Use OctoQuad encoders
 SWERVE_ENCODER_TYPE = SwerveEncoderType.OCTOQUAD

 // Use SRS Hub analog encoders
 SWERVE_ENCODER_TYPE = SwerveEncoderType.ANALOG

    Usage Example:

    
 // Create steering encoder for front-right module
 SwerveEncoder steeringEncoder = SwerveEncoderFactory.createSteeringEncoder(
     octoQuad, srsHub,
     0,           // OctoQuad port
     1,           // SRS Hub analog pin
     0.0,         // Module offset (radians)
     8192         // Counts per revolution (OctoQuad only)
 );

 // Create drive encoder for front-right module
 SwerveEncoder driveEncoder = SwerveEncoderFactory.createDriveEncoder(
     octoQuad, srsHub,
     4,           // OctoQuad port
     -1           // SRS Hub analog pin (-1 if not used)
 );
    See Also:
    SwerveEncoder, OctoQuadSwerveEncoder, AnalogSwerveEncoder

      • SwerveEncoderFactory

    • Method Detail

      • createSteeringEncoder

        public static SwerveEncoder createSteeringEncoder(OctoQuadFWv3 octoQuad,
                                                  SRSHub srsHub,
                                                  int octoQuadPort,
                                                  int analogPin,
                                                  double offsetRadians,
                                                  int countsPerRev)
        Creates a steering encoder based on the configured encoder type.

        This factory method creates the appropriate encoder implementation based on Constants.SWERVE_ENCODER_TYPE.

        Parameters:
        octoQuad - OctoQuad instance (used if type is OCTOQUAD)
        srsHub - SRS Hub instance (used if type is ANALOG)
        octoQuadPort - OctoQuad port number (0-3 for steering, ignored if type is ANALOG)
        analogPin - SRS Hub analog pin number (1-12, ignored if type is OCTOQUAD)
        offsetRadians - encoder offset for zero calibration (radians)
        countsPerRev - counts per revolution (only used for OCTOQUAD, default 8192)
        Returns:
        configured steering encoder

      • createSteeringEncoder

        public static SwerveEncoder createSteeringEncoder(OctoQuadFWv3 octoQuad,
                                                  SRSHub srsHub,
                                                  int octoQuadPort,
                                                  int analogPin,
                                                  double offsetRadians)
        Creates a steering encoder with default counts per revolution (8192).
        Parameters:
        octoQuad - OctoQuad instance (used if type is OCTOQUAD)
        srsHub - SRS Hub instance (used if type is ANALOG)
        octoQuadPort - OctoQuad port number (0-3 for steering, ignored if type is ANALOG)
        analogPin - SRS Hub analog pin number (1-12, ignored if type is OCTOQUAD)
        offsetRadians - encoder offset for zero calibration (radians)
        Returns:
        configured steering encoder

      • createDriveEncoder

        public static SwerveEncoder createDriveEncoder(OctoQuadFWv3 octoQuad,
                                               SRSHub srsHub,
                                               int octoQuadPort,
                                               int analogPin)
        Creates a drive encoder based on the configured encoder type.

        Note: Analog encoders are not recommended for drive wheels. Use OctoQuad digital encoders for accurate velocity measurement. If using ANALOG type, this will return 0 for velocity (drive wheels should use motor encoders instead).

        Parameters:
        octoQuad - OctoQuad instance (used if type is OCTOQUAD)
        srsHub - SRS Hub instance (not used for drive encoders)
        octoQuadPort - OctoQuad port number (4-7 for drive, ignored if type is ANALOG)
        analogPin - SRS Hub analog pin number (not used for drive encoders)
        Returns:
        configured drive encoder

      • getCurrentEncoderType

        public static SwerveEncoderFactory.SwerveEncoderType getCurrentEncoderType()
        Gets the current encoder type from configuration.
        Returns:
        current encoder type
